name: Indonesian Satays
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the beef, pork, and chicken into silver dollar-sized medallions about 1/4" thick.
2. Pound each lightly to flatten them.
3. Insert a bamboo skewer into each medallion, keeping the medallions as flat as possible.
4. Combine the soy sauce, ginger, garlic, pepper, water, and sugar in a small bowl.
5. Arrange the satays in the marinade with their skewers resting on the edge of the bowl.
6. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 3 hours or longer.
7. Wash and trim the green onions. Make several lengthwise cuts through the green ends.
8. Place the green onions, green side down, in a small bowl of ice water. Cover and refrigerate.
9. Preheat the broiler.
10. Arrange the satays on the broiler pan with the skewers all on one side. Cover the skewers with foil.
11. Broil 3 inches from the heat for 3 minutes.
12. Turn the satays and continue broiling until done to taste (roughly 2 more minutes).
13. Remove the chicken and beef satays. Cook the pork satays until well done.
14. Arrange on a board or platter and garnish with the green onions.
15. Serve.
